replies.author: @PoolKid75
replies.text:
​@CD-Gaming 🤦‍♂️ Sega was all flash and no substance, that's why Nintendo is still going strong and Sega is long gone.

But besides that, are you seriously still fighting this war? Lol. You're like that Japanese soldier that was on an island and didn't know WWII had ended 20 years earlier.

replies.author: @PoolKid75
replies.text:
​@CD-Gaming in all seriousness, I never gave Nintendo much thought for the longest time. I was a PlayStation guy. But by the PS3 era I had grown bored and disillusioned with video games. There was nothing being made for me. That's when I discovered what all the Nintendo fuss was about. And as my PS3 sat and collected dust I put thousands of hours into my Nintendo consoles, and now I'm 150% a Nintendo guy.

I'm actively trying to catch up on all the Nintendo I missed out on in the 90s and 2000s. I'm literally playing a Zelda game as I'm writing this lol. There's so much to play it's almost too much lol. But I'm enjoying it, and I encourage anyone to try it.

replies.author: @CD-Gaming
replies.text:
@PoolKid75 They're still making Mega Drive games... The first game I played was Sonic on my late brother's Master System, my first console, which I not only still have but also still works, was my Mega Drive II/Mega CD II Combi! I've been on a bit of a nostalgia binge of late as well, getting a couple of Mega Drive games I didn't play the first time round, like Altered Beasts! Despite that, I've never had a Dreamcast, I nearly had a 32X, but was talked out of it cuz "no games"!

When I get a console, I get if for the games, if there are enough games on it I am interested in! That's why it took me so long to get a PS3, despite my brother telling me I wanted one, when there were only 2 games I was even interested in on it (Metal Gear Solid 4 and Gran Turismo 5), his whole "it plays Blu-Ray" did not sway (plus, I already knew that anyway)! No, for some reason, he had it in his head I was a techy, his argument being "I had all the consoles", despite the considerable number of consoles I've never owned and have no intention of ever getting...

author: @jonazo7188
text: 9:25 I just looked into this today, the original writer for the Zelda movie is a guy named Derek Connolly, who cowrote the original, unproduced script for the third Star Wars sequel. That script was basically completely replaced with Rise of Skywalker (no idea if it would’ve been any better, but you can read the synopsis of it on Wikipedia). In any case, Derek Connolly has been replaced as screenwriter with T.S. Nowlin, a frequent collaborator with the director Wes Ball.

author: @ForemostExpert
text:
I think that’s silly.  My guess is that link has rescued Zelda from the castle and they are on the run.  It looks like he’s teaching her to use a bow.
We need to stop with this shit.  If Aliens had never released and they made that exact film in 2025, and production still leaked of sigourney weaver holding a pulse rifle, jackasses would be screaming “oh no, not another girlboss.”
I understand not wanting to give Hollywood the benefit of the doubt, but Nintendo is heavily involved in this.
Also, it’s not called the Legend of Link.  I’ve always wanted to know more about what makes Zelda a legend.  Hopefully, the movie will have a good balance between link AND Zelda.  Frankly, the more they give Zelda to say and do, the more Link can be the strong SILENT type.  I do not want link to be too talkative.

author: @dragonsigner
text:
There have been games where you can play as PRINCESS ZELDA.  (Hyrule warriors and Zelda echoes of wisdom to name a few).

 She does dress up as a Ninja (shieka) In a few games (ocarina of Time..to name one)

To hide herself from Ganondorf

She can protect herself. She is the reincarnation human form of the goddess Hylia
 She has great powers. 

But link is the main men dude we play who takes on temple and evil. 

My point is .I forsee this movie making $$$$$$$$$$$$$$$

author: @JCardo2502
text: I´m very sure a black actor will be casted as Ganondorf, probably Idris Elba or Terry Crews. If Impa is in this movie I´m guessing she will be an asian (probably a Japanese American or pure Japanese because Impa is kind of like a Shinobi Assassin/Ninja and I don´t think casting a white woman on that role would sit well with anyone). The Gerudos will be certainly black, there´s no doubt about that, the Zora... if we go for actors, I say they can be either whites or asians. The Gorons will be CGI, no way they´ll get actors for Gorons. Kokiris can be white since we´ve seen blonde Kokiris and Redhead Kokiris. Hyrule Castle Citizens can be either white and asians, I don´t really mind either way. same for Kakariko citizens. If more Sheikahs appear on this film I want them to be asians, they give me asian vibes in BotW and TotK and I think it was intentional. The Rito will certainly be CGI, I doubt anyone will but on a bird costume for this film... am I missing any other Zelda race?

author: @ZombieRyushu
text: I don't even understand why it is a Zelda movie is even being produced. Video game to Movie translations RARELY Go well. The stories are too long, to complex, too interwoven to be told in movie form: See: Warcraft, Final Fantasy the Spirits within.

I mean yeah she's going to have her moment's just as Link is going to have his moments. I don't see Nintendo favoring one or the other. I see them being balancing each other. Maybe there will be no main role, but two supporting characters together making the main role.
